pattern: A stack of directory names canonicalizes a Unix path: each real name is pushed, a parent reference pops, and current-directory or empty segments are ignored.
hints: Splitting the path on the slash character turns it into a list of segments, most of which are either real names or special tokens.
Walk the segments keeping a stack: a normal name goes on, an empty piece or a single dot is skipped, and a double dot should undo the most recent name.
After processing, join the stacked names with single slashes and prepend a leading slash; an empty stack means the root, which is just one slash.

edge_inputs.description: parent reference above root stays at root
triple dot is an ordinary directory name
single dot is the current directory

stress.description: random valid absolute paths built from names, dots, and double dots
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    names = ['a', 'b', 'foo', 'bar', '.', '..', '...', 'x1', 'home']
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(40, size)))
    parts = [rng.choice(names) for _ in range(n)]
    sep = ''.join('/' * rng.randint(1, 2) for _ in range(n))
    path = '/'
    for p in parts:
        path += p + '/' * rng.randint(1, 2)
    return [path]
stress.note: double slashes and dot tokens stress the canonicalization rules
